snippets.pull_quote: I'm having a hard time getting out of my own way, doing for me what I do for others.
snippets.website_angle: Mirror the cobbler's-children problem fractional operators know too well.

snippets.pull_quote: I want to call it dead time. I feel like I'm busier than I should be, but I don't have as much client time as I would like.
snippets.website_angle: Name the gap between busy-feeling and billable — operators will recognize it instantly.

snippets.pull_quote: Building a website is just a bitch. It just sucks. And when you know enough to be dangerous, everything you do, you hate.
snippets.website_angle: Validate the perfectionism trap that keeps fractionals invisible to prospects.

snippets.pull_quote: I don't want to automate this uncaring cold message — that's not reflective of what I'm trying to do for my clients.
snippets.website_angle: Position against spray-and-pray outreach; speak to operators who refuse to cheapen their brand.

snippets.pull_quote: The feast or famine cycle for sure. Getting consistent new business without having to work really hard to get the business — that's the magic thing.
snippets.website_angle: Lead with the pipeline anxiety every solo operator feels but rarely admits.

snippets.pull_quote: Brand strategy is where I really like sitting — but most of my projects haven't got to focus on that. It's been more one-off campaigns or events.
snippets.website_angle: Speak to fractionals trapped in tactical work who want to sell at the strategic level.

snippets.pull_quote: I haven't nailed who I want to get to because I'm scared to narrow too far.
snippets.website_angle: Address the ICP avoidance that keeps messaging vague and pipelines thin.

snippets.pull_quote: I'm paying for my own software and picking my babies — ChatGPT pro, Claude pro, HoneyBook, Adobe alternatives… it adds up and it's a time suck.
snippets.website_angle: Highlight the hidden cost of stitching together a solo operator's tech stack.

snippets.pull_quote: Figuring out how I want to set it up and then actually sticking to it is more work than writing a checklist on a piece of paper. It feels like a barrier to entry.
snippets.website_angle: Challenge dashboard-heavy tools; position for operators who need momentum, not more setup.

snippets.pull_quote: It would be a lie to say I feel fulfilled every week. Until I'm hitting that 40-hour mark or have my website done, those things are constantly in the back of my mind.
snippets.website_angle: Connect product value to the emotional baseline fractionals are trying to recover.

snippets.pull_quote: I'm not a salesperson. I'm not the smooth talker in the room. That's definitely a con right now to the whole process.
snippets.website_angle: Reassure operators that great work shouldn't require a sales personality to find clients.

snippets.pull_quote: I was done playing office politics. I wanted a team mentality — not feeling like we were all competing against each other all the time.
snippets.website_angle: Speak to the 'why I went fractional' origin story that resonates with values-driven operators.
